scenarios.title: A reconciliation entry documents the policy reversal
scenarios.summary: The spec 042 audit history file contains a single reconciliation entry whose phase identifier names the policy-reversal source bug, and whose narrative names the binding policy authority, the now-required fail-loud form, and the now-forbidden substitution form.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — proven non-tautological by SCN-042-006-C: a synthetic copy with the reconciliation entry stripped MUST cause the contract test to FAIL RED with a named error.

scenarios.title: Every stale narrative carries a leading supersession marker
scenarios.summary: Every notes / reason / closeReason string in the spec 042 audit history file whose substance praises the now-forbidden substitution form starts with the supersession marker that names the policy-reversal source bug. The original narrative substance is preserved verbatim immediately after the marker.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — proven non-tautological by SCN-042-006-D: a synthetic copy with the leading marker stripped from any one of the 9 affected fields MUST cause the table-driven contract test to FAIL RED with a named-field error.

scenarios.title: Removing the reconciliation entry causes the contract test to fail RED
scenarios.summary: Adversarial mutation test: when the reconciliation entry is removed from a synthetic in-memory copy of the audit history file, the contract validator returns a non-nil error whose message names the missing reconciliation entry. The test PASSES (i.e. correctly rejects the mutated copy) in the post-fix state; the original assertion is that the validator MUST NOT silently accept a mutated copy with the reconciliation entry stripped.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — this scenario IS the adversarial proof for SCN-042-006-A. It uses an in-memory mutation of the live file (no on-disk write); the test asserts the validator's error message names 'reconciliation entry missing' (or equivalent) so a maintainer who silently removes the entry in a future PR will see this test FAIL RED.

scenarios.title: Stripping a supersession marker from any affected narrative causes the contract test to fail RED
scenarios.summary: Adversarial mutation test (table-driven, parameterized over the 9 affected narrative fields): when the leading supersession marker is stripped from any one affected narrative string field in a synthetic in-memory copy of the audit history file, the contract validator returns a non-nil error whose message names the affected field path. Each of the 9 sub-cases independently asserts the validator MUST NOT silently accept a mutated copy missing the marker on its respective field.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — this scenario IS the adversarial proof for SCN-042-006-B. It is table-driven across the 9 fields enumerated in spec.md → 'Stale Audit Lines (Evidence)' table; each sub-case independently mutates ONE field's marker and asserts the validator rejects the mutated copy with a named-field error. A maintainer who silently strips a single marker in a future PR will see exactly that one sub-case FAIL RED, pinning the regression to the affected field.
